title: Clintonia borealis
taxonomy: Clintonia borealis is classified within the kingdom Plantae, specifically within the phylum Liliopsida, and further classified as a member of the family Asparagaceae, and the genus Clintonia. This classification places it within the group of flowering plants. Within the Asparagaceae family, Clintonia borealis is part of the order Asparagales. The Asparagales order is a diverse group of flowering plants that also includes the lily and agave families. Clintonia borealis is further classified as a species within the Clintonia genus, which is a small group of flowering plants native to North America.
uses: Clintonia borealis, also known as the yellowClintonia or yellow star-grass, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing swelling and protecting against cell damage. Its roots and leaves are said to be used in various remedies, including those for skin conditions and respiratory issues. However, more research is needed to fully understand and confirm the plant's medicinal uses. Traditionally, the plant is also used as a treatment for various ailments, including fever and colds.

parts: Clintonia borealis, also known as the bluebead lily, is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine by indigenous communities in North America.
